items.title: Today at Royal Ascot: Bow Echo and Gstaad lock horns in St James's Palace Stakes
items.summary: Royal Ascot kicks off on Tuesday with a stellar renewal of the Queen Anne Stakes and a battle of the Guineas winners in the St James's Palace Stakes.

items.title: Thomas Tuchel can have no excuses as England get World Cup underway – make the semi-finals at least or he has failed
items.summary: THERE can be no excuses for Thomas Tuchel when England’s World Cup gets underway in Dallas tomorrow. No get-out. No leeway. Tuchel’s job is simple. It’s semi- finals, at least, or failure. A big ask? Maybe. But that’s what you get paid the big bucks for. After all, Sir Gareth Southgate was condemned

items.title: Iran fight back twice to rescue thrilling draw against New Zealand
items.summary: Iran put their turbulent build-up to the World Cup behind them as they came from behind twice to secure a 2-2 draw with New Zealand in what was arguably the game of the tournament so far.

items.title: Iran v Iran in the stands as politics and football intertwine
items.summary: The Iranian team may want to unite people but what the game with New Zealand revealed is just how divided many Iranians remain, writes BBC North America correspondent Shaimaa Khalil.

items.title: Infantino using private jet in attempt to watch two World Cup matches per day
items.summary: Fifa president sets gruelling itinerary across tournament World Cup to generate about 9m tons of carbon dioxide Gianni Infantino is planning to attend two World Cup games each day where possible for the rest of the tournament despite the huge distances involved. items.title: Bleep tests, alcohol bans and Gazza: Italia 90 set the bar for England and sports science | Sean Ingle
items.summary: Bobby Robson employed a head of human performance for the World Cup and, despite wariness, the players got on board The eve of Italia 90.
